texto: Suffering has its place in the scheme of things. It serves a purpose even though the sufferer may be entirely free from the taint of sin... Among the gentlest and sweetest souls are sometimes found those who suffer much. A person that accepts his lot, does his best, loving both God and man, is a success and will experience a joy in living.
explicacao: Citação de Joseph F. Merrill sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Suffering has its place in the scheme of things. It serves a purpose even though the sufferer may…” articula vida, joy, suffering em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como vida e suffering.
